title: Wire plexus.docs into Console chat agent
description:
The Console chat agent (built-in procedure `builtin:console/chat`) is the
LLM-facing surface that users interact with through the web app's
`/lab/console`. After the documentation KB cleanup, the assistant's
system prompt still doesn't mention the new progressive-disclosure
docs primitives.

## Behavior

Given the Console chat agent receives a question about how something in
Plexus works (for example "how do I author a score YAML?", "how do I
run a feedback evaluation?", "what does plexus.evaluation.run accept?"),
the assistant should consult `plexus.docs.list()` to find the right
topic id and `plexus.docs.get({ id = "..." })` to read the body before
answering, rather than relying on its own training data.

## Acceptance

- The built-in console chat agent's system prompt teaches the assistant
  about `plexus.docs.list()` and `plexus.docs.get({ id = "..." })`,
  including the available namespaces and the canonical
  `mcp.execute-tactus-overview` topic.
- The procedure version is bumped so the new prompt rolls out.
- A regression test confirms the prompt mentions the docs primitives.
